element_choice.justification: Excellent use of <time datetime>, <pre><code>, <article>, <aside>, <dl> for metadata, and <ol> for ordered comments. The one minor issue: <address> (line 150) wraps a 'More articles by Priya Sharma' link — this is a slight semantic stretch since <address> is for contact information, not a content discovery link, even if it's author-related.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA used precisely where native HTML doesn't provide a label: aria-labelledby on <article> and all <section> elements, aria-label on <nav> and <aside>, aria-hidden on decorative asterisks, and aria-describedby for the email hint. No redundant role attributes anywhere.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Single h1 for the article title, h2 for all article sections and top-level page sections (Related Articles, Comments, About the author), and h3 for nested content (related article titles, comment author headings, Leave a comment). No skipped levels throughout.

landmark_structure.justification: All key landmarks present and correctly scoped: <header>/<footer> outside <main>, <main> wrapping only primary content, <nav aria-label> for both primary and footer navs, <aside> for pull quote, <section aria-labelledby> throughout. Skip links for both main content and comments are the first focusable elements.

content_realism.justification: Fully realistic content: detailed narrative article text with specific metrics (14 API calls, 38% load time reduction), real-feeling author bio with job title and employer, realistic related article titles with plausible authors and dates, and substantive comment text that engages with the article.

form_semantics.justification: All inputs have explicit <label> elements; name/email fields grouped under <fieldset>/<legend>; appropriate type attributes (text, email); autocomplete attributes on both fields; aria-describedby associates the email hint; required indicated on all fields.

list_semantics.justification: Nav items in <ul>, related article cards in <ul> (count matters), comments in <ol> (temporal order is meaningful). All appropriate list types chosen correctly.

expectations_met: <main> wraps article, related articles, and comments; site header and footer are outside it
Skip links ('Skip to main content', 'Skip to comments') are the first focusable elements
Article sections use h2 headings within the article
Pull quote uses <aside aria-label='Pull quote'> without <blockquote>
Related articles section uses h2 and article cards in a <ul>
Comment author names use <h3>, dates use <time>
Heading hierarchy is consistent: h1 → h2 → h3, no skipped levels
expectations_missed: <address> (line 150) wraps a 'More articles by Priya Sharma' link — contact info semantics are a stretch for a content discovery link, even in an author bio context
additional_observations: Use of <dl> for article metadata (author, published date, topic) is an excellent semantic choice
Comments correctly placed in <ol> rather than <ul>, reflecting temporal ordering
The comment textarea sits outside the <fieldset> intentionally, grouping only the personal details fields — a reasonable design decision
The form's aria-labelledby pointing to the same heading as its enclosing <section> is mildly redundant but harmless
